message:
feat(pane-terminal): add multiplexer capability matrix with fallback policy (bd-6u66i)

Add PaneCapabilityMatrix derived from TerminalCapabilities that resolves
pane-specific feature availability per mux environment (None/Tmux/Screen/Zellij).
Includes drag_enabled(), focus_cancel_effective(), and structured limitation
reporting. GNU Screen disables mouse drag and focus events; other muxes
preserve full pane interaction. 8 new tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1540,7 +1540,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-6sqaq","title":"Diagram Search & Filter (highlight/dim nodes)","description":"## Goal
Add search functionality within rendered diagrams. Type / to open search bar, filter nodes by label text. Matching nodes highlight, non-matching nodes dim to 50% opacity. Press Enter to cycle through matches.

## Why
- Makes large diagrams navigable — find the node you care about without scanning the entire diagram.
- Builds on the Buffer's opacity stack (scissor/opacity already supported).
- Simple, well-understood UX pattern (Ctrl+F in any editor).
- Combines well with interactive navigation (idea #3): search finds nodes, navigation explores from there.

## Features
1. / opens search input bar (bottom of diagram area)
2. As user types, nodes with matching labels highlight (accent border + full opacity)
3. Non-matching nodes dim to 50% opacity
4. Non-matching edges dim to 30% opacity
5. Enter cycles to next match, Shift+Enter to previous
6. Esc clears search and restores full opacity
7. Match count displayed: "3/12 nodes match"
8. Can filter by: label text (default), shape type (:diamond), cluster name (@cluster)

## Implementation
- In MermaidShowcaseState: search_active, search_query, search_matches
- During rendering: apply opacity multiplier to non-matching nodes/edges
- Use existing Buffer opacity stack for dimming

## Dependencies
- Interactive node navigation (bd-w5vn5) for shared selection state

## Files
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_showcase.rs (search UI + state)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (opacity-based dimming for non-matches)

## Tests
- Unit: search "foo" matches nodes containing "foo"
- Unit: shape filter ":diamond" matches only diamond nodes
- Snapshot: diagram with search active showing highlighted matches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CobaltHollow","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:49.300320216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:01.199238132Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:01.199218325Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["interactivity","mermaid","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6sqaq","depends_on_id":"bd-w5vn5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.555460062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":807,"issue_id":"bd-6sqaq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Committed diagram search & filter (114845d3). Search input captures chars, matches nodes by id/label, highlights current match via SelectionState, dims non-matching node cells to ~33% brightness using viewport-aware bounding box math. Search bar at bottom shows query + [n/m] count. Enter/n/N cycle matches, Backspace edits, Esc exits. 157 mermaid showcase tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:45:56Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-70rmj","title":"Smart Edge Bundling for Dense Graphs","description":"## Goal
Bundle parallel edges between the same node groups to reduce visual clutter on dense graphs. Instead of 5 separate edges between two subgraphs, show a single thick bundled edge with "×5" label.

## Motivation
Microservice architectures and complex dependency graphs often have many edges between the same clusters. Individual edge rendering creates a "hairball" that's unreadable. Edge bundling is the standard solution in graph visualization.

## Algorithm
1. Post-layout, group edges by (source_cluster, target_cluster) or (source_node, target_node).
2. For groups with 3+ parallel edges: replace with a single bundled edge.
3. Bundle edge: thicker line (or double line), label showing "×N", color blended from constituent edges.
4. For 2 parallel edges: keep separate but offset slightly to avoid overlap.
5. Self-referencing bundles (within same cluster): render as a curved loop.

## Integration Points
- Runs as a post-processing step after layout, before rendering.
- Operates on RoutedEdge list from mermaid_layout.rs.
- Bundling is optional (config flag) — some users want to see all individual edges.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs (add bundle_edges post-processing)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (render bundled edges with thickness + label)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (add bundling config to MermaidConfig)

## Tests
- Unit: 5 parallel A→B edges become 1 bundle with "×5"
- Unit: 2 parallel edges kept separate but offset
- Snapshot: dense graph before/after bundling","notes":"CopperHawk: Added 18 comprehensive edge bundling tests (13 layout + 5 renderer). Implementation was already complete. Tests verify: disabled config, single edge, threshold, direction separation, arrow separation, non-parallel preservation, canonical index, LR offset, min_count clamp, sorted members, self-loop safety, non-graph skip, multi-group counts, bundle_count_label format, thick line style, label appending. All 21 bundle tests pass, clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CopperHawk","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:28:44.020939115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:36:39.164222914Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:36:39.164134850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-70rmj","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:17.886630978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":801,"issue_id":"bd-70rmj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCreek claiming. Will implement smart edge bundling: post-layout grouping of parallel edges, bundle rendering with thickness + ×N labels, and config flag.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:51:11Z"},{"id":853,"issue_id":"bd-70rmj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented Mermaid edge bundling plumbing:
- Post-layout groups parallel edges and offsets/bundles them (min count configurable).
- Bundled edges render thicker and append a ×N label; selection highlighting propagates across bundle members.
- Updated minimap tests for new LayoutEdgePath fields; also fixed SpatialHitIndex cache dirty bookkeeping.

Commit: d42a3580.","created_at":"2026-02-06T09:09:20Z"}]}
